title: Why file-based routing with a page per section?

blocks.body: Each section could be served by a single dynamic `[slug]/page.tsx` route, but having a file per section gives full control over per-page metadata and loading behavior. It also makes the project structure self-documenting -- you can see every section by listing the directories under `app/[locale]/`. The small amount of boilerplate per page (six lines that are identical except for the slug) is an acceptable tradeoff for this clarity.
